Follow below standard 
AI Mode conversation: C# codeing standards in pointsYou said: C# coding standards in pointsC# coding standards in pointsC# coding standards ensure code readability, maintainability, and seamless team collaboration by aligning development practices with industry norms.The primary guidelines established by the Microsoft .NET Coding Conventions and broader industry best practices are categorized below:Naming Conventions & CasingPascalCase for classes, structs, enums, properties, methods, and namespaces.camelCase for method arguments, local variables, and inline parameters.Underscore prefix (_camelCase) for private and protected instance fields.Static prefix (s_camelCase) strictly for private static fields.Interface prefix using a capital I followed by PascalCase (e.g., IUserRepository).Boolean prefixes using terms like Is, Has, or Can (e.g., isValid, hasAccess).Avoid abbreviations unless they are universally recognized globally (e.g., use xmlDocument, not xmlDoc).Avoid Hungarian notation or encoding data type prefixes into variable
